title: 蒋方舟学位被撤销，全网都在狂欢，而我却感受到一丝悲哀 | 原文
content: Renmin University revoked writer Jiang Fangzhou's master's degree on July 13, 2026, citing nine instances of uncopied text overlap with a foreign journal paper. This reversed an earlier finding of mere "academic impropriety" without misconduct. The author expresses sadness amid public celebration of her downfall, noting the case reflects broader issues in China's academic accountability rather than personal triumph.

content: Graphify is a Python-based AI coding assistant skill compatible with Claude Code, Codex, OpenCode, Cursor, and Gemini CLI. It converts folders containing code, SQL schemas, R scripts, shell scripts, documents, papers, images, or videos into a unified, queryable knowledge graph. The tool integrates application code, database schemas, and infrastructure into a single graph structure. The repository has 83,695 stars and 8,260 forks.
source: Trending repositories on GitHub today · GitHub - Graphify-Labs

title: Before Blockchains, There Was State Machine Replication
content: A podcast features MIT's Barbara Liskov, a Turing Award winner, and a16z's Ittai Abraham discussing how 1980s replication techniques—viewstamped replication, PBFT, and state machine replication—became foundational for modern blockchain protocols. Hosted by Tim Roughgarden, the conversation covers the evolution of distributed systems research, theory-practice relationships, modularity, and AI's impact on systems research.

title: claude 的 workflow 收益是否真的很大，是否是阻止转 gpt 的最大因素
content: A developer primarily uses Claude over Codex for coding due to Claude's adversarial review workflow catching bugs and its familiar terminal UI, though they question whether the bug fixes are truly critical. They run Opus 4.8 xhigh mode, finding Codex's 5.6 SOL ultra-high thinking mode too costly. They also discuss terminal preferences: switched from iTerm2 to Ghostty for better experience, but Ghostty has a severe Chinese input bug in TUIs that deletes characters. They avoided Warp due to integration bloat and commercial conflicts with developer needs.
eventKey: claude-workflow-收益是否真的很大-是否是阻止转-gpt-的最大因素

title: 动力电池新国标落地：“不起火”硬门槛背后，成本“拉锯战”成为关键
content: New Chinese EV battery safety standards taking effect July 2026 mandate no fire or explosion for 2 hours after thermal runaway, replacing the previous 5-minute warning requirement. The rules add bottom impact and fast-charging cycle tests. While major manufacturers like CATL and BYD have already certified compliance, smaller battery makers face 15-20% higher production costs and potential market exit. Cost pressures are reshaping supply chains, with automakers diversifying suppliers. Industry consolidation of 30-40% of small cell producers is anticipated. Standards apply only to new vehicles, not existing stock.
eventKey: 动力电池新国标落地-“不起火”硬门槛背后-成本“拉锯战”成为关键
normalizedTitle: 动力电池新国标落地 “不起火”硬门槛背后 成本“拉锯战”成为关键

title: lululemon「割不动」中国人了
content: Lululemon is losing its grip on Chinese consumers amid growing backlash against its "cult-like" community marketing. A rain-soaked Shanghai yoga event in June drew social media ridicule, damaging the brand's carefully crafted elite identity. The company faces compounding crises: a "China Great Wall drum" controversy, recurring fabric quality issues, and a 43% stock decline in 2026. Founder Chip Wilson, who built lululemon on "Super Girl" exclusivity and premium pricing, has publicly criticized current management for diluting the brand through overexpansion into mass markets, excessive SKUs, and political correctness. While China remains a growth engine with 30% Q1 revenue increases, competition from Alo Yoga and local brand MAIA ACTIVE is intensifying, and growth rates are decelerating. The Americas market has turned negative, with same-store sales down 5%. Lululemon is now turning to India, though cultural
eventKey: lululemon「割不动」中国人了
normalizedTitle: lululemon「割不动」中国人了

title: 微信“小绿书”会是下一个内容风口吗？
content:
WeChat rebranded its "image-text" feature as "Tie Tu" (pictured posts) in March, pushing short-form, image-heavy content resembling Xiaohongshu across multiple entry points. The platform is actively boosting distribution, with Tie Tu posts reportedly achieving 3-5x higher average views than traditional long articles and 40% longer user dwell time.

The shift responds to declining public account engagement and users migrating to lighter content platforms. Tie Tu advantages include higher completion rates, easier algorithmic distribution, and lower creation barriers enabling frequent posting. However, traffic-dependent monetization risks instability if platform priorities shift.

Key challenges persist: weak community interaction due to limited commenting features, prevalent content reposting from Xiaohongshu, and shallow, clickbait-style posts dominating. The fundamental tension remains whether WeChat can build genuine community atmosphere around short-form content while preserving its long-form depth, or if algorithm-driven short content will ultimately displace substantive
eventKey: 微信“小绿书”会是下一个内容风口吗
normalizedTitle: 微信“小绿书”会是下一个内容风口吗

title: 百度和腾讯，开始抢打工人的开机画面
content: Baidu and Tencent are competing to dominate the AI-powered workplace entry point. Tencent's WorkBuddy integrates AI into WeChat/Enterprise WeChat group chats, capturing where tasks originate. Baidu's DuMate takes a different approach, focusing on end-to-end task execution across applications, delivering finished work products directly. Baidu founder Robin Li proposes "Daily Active Agents" (DAA) as the key metric for AI success—measuring agents that complete real tasks. With its full-stack "chip-cloud-model-agent" ecosystem, Baidu aims to eliminate tedious manual work, while Tencent leverages its social connectivity优势.
eventKey: 百度和腾讯-开始抢打工人的开机画面
normalizedTitle: 百度和腾讯 开始抢打工人的开机画面
